title: Draba arseniewii
intro: Draba arseniewii, also known as Arseniev's whitlow grass, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae. The plant is rich in health benefits, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of Draba arseniewii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Draba arseniewii is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in the form of creams and ointments to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds.
taxonomy: Draba arseniewii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as mustard or cabbage family. This plant is a member of the genus Draba, which consists of various species of small, often alpine plants. The classification of Draba arseniewii is further detailed as a member of the tribe Drabeae within the family Brassicaceae. At the kingdom level, Draba arseniewii belongs to the Plantae kingdom. Further classification at the phylum level places it in the Magnoliophyta phylum.
